verdict_reasoning: LYN (Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase) is anchored exclusively to the inner (cytoplasmic) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-myristoylation and palmitoylation. It has no transmembrane domain, no extracellular domain, and no GPI anchor. Its entire protein body — SH4, SH3, SH2, and kinase domains — faces the cytoplasm. Although LYN associates with surface receptors such as BCR, FcεRI, and CD19, these are cytoplasmic, reversible, wash-sensitive interactions. No extracellular binder of any modality can access LYN from outside the cell. pMHC is not applicable here. Inner-leaflet-anchored verdict is unambiguous.
